critical_debug_context.wormbot_fix_note: sys.path injection in wormbot.py (the bridge) inserts WORMBOT_MODS so absolute imports from base_module resolve correctly. If new worm_bot language modules are added ‚Äî ensure they use 'from base_module import BaseModule', not relative.
critical_debug_context.drop_zone_note: reregister_drop() is the single re-registration path after every drop. It lives at line 103 of scroll_drop.py. The inline duplicate is gone. If drop zone ever degrades again ‚Äî check reregister_drop() is still being called from _on_drop via parent.after(200).

surgical_reminders: chmod u+w <file> before patching sealed files
guard seal confirm after every verified change
ast.parse() after every single write ‚Äî no exceptions
Test and verify BEFORE sealing ‚Äî never seal untested patches
grep first, sed to read, python3 to patch
Never rewrite working files from scratch
Three-place trigger registration: MODULE_TRIGGERS + _triggers + guard scope
NEVER reduce guard scope ‚Äî ui/ coverage is deliberate
cat -A to diagnose byte corruption ‚Äî em-dashes and newlines break heredoc string matching
Use binary rb/wb read/write when em-dash or special chars are in the target string
lesson_learned_042: Em-dash characters (‚îÄ and ‚Äî) embedded in Python heredocs via << 'EOF' get mangled into multi-byte sequences (\xe2\x94\x80 etc). String match fails silently with 'got 0'. Always use cat -A to read the raw bytes first, then either patch with binary rb/wb mode using exact byte sequences, or use index-based slicing (find landmark, slice, replace). Never trust visual match when special characters are in the target.
